channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: PS5 Exclusive Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls Adds Two More Avengers To Its Starting Roster
item.content:encoded:
The drip-feed of reveals for the spiritual successor to Marvel vs. Capcom continued this weekend at EVO Japan. Sony and developer Arc Systems took the opportunity to announce two new additions to the roster for Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls, and they probably won’t be surprising anyone.

Savage Hulk and Shuri as Black Panther will be rounding out the Avengers side of the fighting game’s initial 20-character roster. It’s Savage Hulk because in Tokon, S.H.I.E.L.D. and Captain America have “exiled” Hulk to the Savage Land because of his “overwhelming power and instability.” Hence, the dinosaur bone armor. Hulk will have a Gamma Gauge that players build up during the course of a match to enter Gamma Rage mode and deploy extra powerful one-shot attacks.

Shuri’s backstory, meanwhile, has her only recently having taken on the mantle of Black Panther and become queen of Wakanda. Unlike Hulk, she’ll be fast and agile, with long range thanks to moves utilizing the Spear of Bashenga. She’ll also utilize a Bast’s Blessing mode to deash in multiple directions across the map to close the distance. I’m not sold on either character’s costume, but swappable skins, and potentially paid ones, will no doubt be part of the mix.

Joining Captain America and Iron Man, the Avengers side of the roster for Marvel Tokon is now complete. The Guardians and X-Men squads are too, leaving a number of villains on the table that could still be confirmed for the last six characters to be revealed. While Doctor Doom has been confirmed, Big bads like Thanos, Apocalypse, and Carnage are all still on the table. The end of the teaser trailer above certainly has fans hoping the Cletus Kasady/Symbiote duo is part of the mix.

Marvel Tokon is also getting four extra characters via DLC. Set to release on August 6, right after Spider-Man: Brand New Day and before Avengers: Doomsday, we’ll see if the remaining reveals end up tying into either of those tentpoles.



https://kotaku.com/ps5-exclusive-marvel-tokon-fighting-souls-adds-two-more-avengers-to-its-starting-roster-2000692787
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ex-GTA Producer Takes A Break From MindsEye Mess To Help Someone Vibe-Code A Game
item.content:encoded:
Mark Gerhard and Leslie Benzies, the two co-CEOs of Build a Rocket Boy, recently decided to take some time out of their busy schedules, which usually consists of telling people they’re redundant via LinkedIn posts and allegedly using spyware to snoop on their employees, to “vibe-code” some new “in-game voice assistant” for MindsEye. 

Well, technically, ElevenLabs and the BARB co-CEOs actually helped vibe-code some nonsense for Arcadia, which is a “unique feature” within MindsEye. And by “unique feature,” I mean user-generated content. The AI-coded feature they managed to create–and by “they” I mean the AI–was “Arcadia Voice,” which is just… voice control. Did they really need an AI’s help for this? Congratulations, though, fellas, because now MindsEye can do something the Xbox Kinect could do back in 2010.

I vibe-coded a AAA game with the creator of GTA, the former CEO of Runescape (@laughivore), and their game studio’s Head of AI (@willgdjones).

We built an in-game voice assistant that understands the game state, can spawn weapons and objects, and even lets you create new objects… https://t.co/Xs2QRS0Gk6 pic.twitter.com/7TRemLD34K

— Joe Reeve – 🇬🇧/acc (@isnit0) April 30, 2026

However, there is one extremely impressive thing about ElevenLab’s video; it’s so unanimously hated that even the MindsEye community on Discord thinks it’s cringe. These are folks who actively enjoy the game MindsEye, by the way, so if they think this sucks, I think we may have truly reached the bottom of the barrel with this one.

In fact, the reaction from the 20 people who do still play the game was so terrible that Build a Rocket Boy’s new Head of AI, Will Jones, posted a lengthy statement on X in an attempt to quell the upset.

“We know that these will never replace the craft of developers, designers, artists, QA, or performers who care about what they are building (in fact, the coding agent is only useful because of the powerful system and beautiful assets already made by our colleagues),” reads part of Jones’ X post. “Games are creative, collaborative, and deeply human.”

Funnily enough, Jones also noted later in the statement that he is “new to game development,” due to the fact that Build a Rocket Boy is the first job he’s had in the gaming industry. However, I’d argue that Jones isn’t new to game development at all. In actuality, he’s new to asking an AI to develop video games for him. It would sort of be like if I said I’d got a new job in the culinary arts, because I’d just microwaved my very first ready meal.



https://kotaku.com/ex-gta-producer-takes-a-break-from-mindseye-mess-to-help-someone-vibe-code-a-game-2000692688
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
